nonpromotion_countermodel.conclusion: contiguous order four alone does not imply arbitrary-column order four; the lower initial-minor signs are essential

proof_boundary: This artifact proves arbitrary-column signed-Hankel signs through order four from the completed contiguous signs and a published strict-total-positivity criterion. It does not prove contiguous order five, PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda<=0.

rows.claim: The actual lambda-zero Xi sequence has the strict signed contiguous signs through order four at every shift.
rows.formula: epsilon_k*H_(k,s)(0)>0 for every s>=0 and k=1,2,3,4
rows.proof_boundary: Uses completed all-shift theorems, not finite evidence.

rows.claim: Reverse a finite block of Hankel columns to convert the signed orientation into ordinary total positivity.
rows.proof_boundary: Finite 4 by (N+1) block for arbitrary N.

rows.claim: Every solid minor of the reversed block is a signed contiguous Hankel minor.
rows.proof_boundary: Exact index map and column-reversal sign.

rows.claim: All initial minors of every reversed finite block are strictly positive.
rows.formula: all solid minors are positive, hence all initial minors are positive
rows.proof_boundary: Requires every lower signed contiguous layer through the block row count.

rows.claim: The rectangular Gasca-Pena criterion promotes positive initial minors to strict total positivity.
rows.formula: all initial minors of an m by p real matrix are positive iff the matrix is strictly totally positive

rows.claim: Every reversed 4 by (N+1) Xi Hankel block is strictly totally positive.
rows.formula: B^(n,N) is strictly totally positive for every n,N>=0
rows.proof_boundary: Only four consecutive Hankel rows are used.

rows.claim: An arbitrary increasing Hankel column set is the reverse of an increasing column set in the finite block.
rows.proof_boundary: Choose N at least the largest requested column offset.

rows.claim: Every arbitrary-column four-row reshaped-Hankel determinant has the required positive sign at lambda zero.
rows.proof_boundary: Complete arbitrary-column order four for consecutive rows.

rows.claim: At every fixed order m, strict signed contiguous signs through m imply all arbitrary-column signed signs through m.
rows.formula: [epsilon_k H_(k,s)>0 for 1<=k<=m, all s] => [epsilon_k R_(k,n)(j_1,...,j_k)>0 for 1<=k<=m]
rows.proof_boundary: A finite-dimensional theorem for each fixed m; it supplies no new contiguous signs.

rows.claim: Contiguous order four alone cannot replace the complete family of lower initial-minor signs.

diagnostics.conclusion: contiguous order four alone does not imply arbitrary-column order four; the lower initial-minor signs are essential

rows.proof_boundary: Exact rational countermodel; it does not concern the actual Xi sequence.

rows.claim: The first new signed-Hankel layer is now contiguous order five; once it is proved, arbitrary columns follow automatically from the fixed-order transfer.
rows.formula: next target: epsilon_5*H_(5,n)(0)=H_(5,n)(0)>0 for every n>=0
rows.proof_boundary: Not an order-five theorem, PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda<=0.

source_contract.order3_source_status: exact all-column reshaped-Hankel order-two and order-three theorem at lambda=0 with order four open
source_contract.order4_source_status: all-shift contiguous order-four forward invariance through lambda zero
source_contract.signed_contiguous_orders: epsilon_k*H_(k,s)(0)>0 for every s>=0 and k=1,2,3,4

status: exact arbitrary-column reshaped-Hankel order-four theorem at lambda zero
